conjecture_title: Polynomial degree-bounding function for hereditary degree-bounded classes
paper_title: Induced $C_4$-free subgraphs with large average degree
summary: The conjecture was answered affirmatively by Girão and Hunter in a 2025 paper in the International Mathematics Research Notices, which proves that every hereditary degree-bounded class has a polynomial degree-bounding function, fully resolving the question left open in arXiv:2307.08361. A related earlier result by Bourneuf, Bucćić, Cook, and Davies (arXiv:2311.03341, published in Advances in Combinatorics 2024) proved polynomial bounds for hereditary classes defined by excluding induced subdivisions of a fixed graph, establishing a key step toward the full result.

since_posted.title: On polynomial degree-boundedness
since_posted.authors: Romain Bourneuf, Matija Bucćić, Linda Cook, James Davies
since_posted.claim: Proves that for every graph H there is a polynomial p such that every graph of average degree at least p(s) contains either K_{s,s} or an induced subdivision of H, establishing polynomial bounds for hereditary degree-bounded classes defined by forbidden induced subdivisions.

since_posted.title: Induced Subdivisions in $K_{s,s}$-Free Graphs With Polynomial Average Degree
since_posted.claim: Proves that every hereditary degree-bounded class has a polynomial degree-bounding function, fully resolving the open question from arXiv:2307.08361.

internal_refs_verified.note: This paper (Nguyen, Scott, Seymour 2025) disproves the weak coarse Menger conjecture and has no connection to polynomial degree-bounding functions for hereditary graph classes; the internal reference is a false match from the corpus extraction.

search_queries: hereditary degree-bounded polynomial degree-bounding function graph theory 2307.08361
"degree-bounding" "hereditary" "polynomial" tau-bounded graphs Du Girão Hunter McCarty Scott
notes: The conjecture is solved: Girão and Hunter (IMRN 2025, DOI 10.1093/imrn/rnaf025) proved polynomial bounds suffice for every hereditary degree-bounded class. The arXiv preprint ID for the Girão-Hunter paper was not found in the sources consulted. The internal reference arXiv:2508.14332 is a false corpus match unrelated to this conjecture. The survey arXiv:2403.05737 (Girão, Hunter, 2024) provides additional context on the degree-boundedness landscape.
